condensed_reasoning:
## Research

I ran a single `mcp__research__research` call to determine whether a new round of direct in-person Ukraine-Russia peace talks would occur in the **July 11-24, 2026** window. The nested research agent's `AskNews` real-time tooling failed (403 billing/subscription errors), so it fell back to `WebSearch` and `WebFetch` against dated articles. This produced a decisive, well-sourced picture.

## Key Evidence

- **No date announced:** As of July 13-14, 2026, no specific date had been announced for a next direct in-person round, and no reporting indicated one was scheduled for or had occurred within the window. The process remained **paused**.
- **Turkey (July 13, 2026):** A Turkish government source told RIA Novosti that Ankara expects talks to resume in Istanbul **"before the end of the summer"** — active contacts, but no date.
- **Russia (June 23, 2026):** Putin signaled readiness on the Istanbul (2022) framework and "realities on the ground," but proposed no date. A **Financial Times report (~July 5, 2026)** said Russia is unlikely to engage substantively **before ~February 2027**, expecting to occupy all of Donetsk and for the US to pressure Ukraine.
- **Ukraine (June 26, 2026):** No plans to resume on the Russian-proposed Istanbul framework; early-July diplomatic focus was the **US/NATO track** (Zelensky-Trump contacts, NATO summit).
- **Base rate:** 2025 saw three direct Istanbul rounds (May 16, June 2, July 23) plus reported early-2026 rounds (Abu Dhabi/Geneva, Jan-Feb). During active phases, rounds land roughly every several weeks to months, giving a ~20-30% chance of one falling in a given ~2-week window.

## Conclusion

I structured five factors: a **~25% base-rate anchor** (logit -1.1), sharply adjusted downward because the process is explicitly paused with **~3 of ~10 window days already elapsed** and no lead-up visible. The dominant negatives were "process paused / no date / convening lead time not started" (logit -1.6) and "FT says Russia waits until ~Feb 2027 / Ukraine focused on US-NATO" (logit -1.4), partly offset by a single positive factor (both sides signal readiness in principle, active Trump/Turkey mediation, low resolution bar — logit +0.4). A qualifying round would require a rapid, unheralded convening within days. Status quo (NO) is strongly favored. My `mcp__premortem__premortem` check returned **approve**, landing independently at ~5-8%. **Final estimate: 6% (logit -2.75).**

capability_gaps: A working real-time news feed for the exact July 11-14 window (AskNews was down); a prediction-market lookup tool was not used for external calibration.
subtle_bugs: The final Zelensky-position WebSearch returned mostly 2025 material and could not confirm the July-2026 'no plans to resume' claim, yet that framing was still used as a firm negative factor — a mild evidence-strength overstatement not surfaced in the final rationale.

classification.approach: Qualitative status-of-negotiations analysis with a base-rate anchor for a ~2-week event window, adjusted down for a paused process.
classification.key_uncertainty: Whether a surprise US/Turkey-mediated convening or an upgraded humanitarian/prisoner meeting could produce a qualifying face-to-face delegation session within the remaining ~7-10 days despite no announced date.

risk_flags.evidence: No prediction-market check (Polymarket/Metaculus/Manifold) was attempted for a resolvable date-window political question, which could have provided an external calibration anchor.

The final WebSearch (Zelensky position) explicitly noted it could NOT confirm the 'no plans to resume' claim from July 2026 and that results were dominated by 2025 material ('the search results don't contain explicit statements from July 2026 about abandoning Istanbul talks'). The agent nonetheless carried the June 26 'no plans' framing forward as strong evidence without flagging this weaker corroboration.

Several load-bearing data points are from June (Putin June 23; Ukraine 'no plans' June 26) or July 5 (FT), with only the Turkey 'before end of summer' item dated July 13. AskNews being down meant the July 11-14 window itself was thinly covered, so an early-window development could have been missed.

strengths: Correctly avoided a lazy 50%: anchored at ~25% base rate then justified a sharp downward move to 6% with two strong, distinctly-sourced negative factors rather than narrative sentiment.
Explicitly incorporated the compressed timeline — ~3 of ~10 window days already elapsed with no talks and no visible lead-up — into the convening-lead-time factor.

Engaged directly with the low resolution bar (any face-to-face delegation session counts) via a genuine positive factor (+0.4) and an adversarial premortem counterargument, rather than dismissing YES outright.
Premortem verification cross-checked each factor against the trace and caught that the 'Ukrainian negotiators view autumn as more likely' sub-claim was not cleanly supported.
weaknesses: Relied on a single research call; with AskNews down, no compensating targeted search specifically covered the July 11-14 window for breaking developments, leaving a small blind spot at the front of the window.
Did not consult any prediction market for an external anchor on a cleanly-resolvable date-window question.
Carried forward the 'Ukraine has no plans to resume' and 'autumn more likely' framings with more weight than the primary sources cleanly supported, as the premortem itself flagged.
Some source domains (turkey.news-pravda.com, 8am.media, wn.com) are lower-tier; the FT and PBS/Time claims were second-hand via aggregators rather than fetched from primary outlets.

issues: AskNews MCP tool failed on all 4 calls: first with 403000 (n_articles>10 reserved for higher tiers), then 403011 (subscription not active / billing). This degraded real-time coverage of the immediate July 11-14 window, forcing reliance on web search and aggregator articles.
Minor token waste: 4 failed AskNews retries before pivoting.
